patch.value: Manual fleet testing found that pm ops policy --repos /path/a /path/b only processed the first repo because remaining path tokens were not folded into the repo list. Also found pm-ops still declared Node >=20 while the pm CLI fleet requires >=22.18.0. Scope: preserve extra repo args, add regression coverage, align package/extension version and Node engine, refresh pm workspace directories, and re-run real fleet policy/test checks.
Fix pm-ops multi-repo path parsing and toolchain alignment

Manual fleet testing found that pm ops policy --repos /path/a /path/b only processed the first repo because remaining path tokens were not folded into the repo list. Also found pm-ops still declared Node >=20 while the pm CLI fleet requires >=22.18.0. Scope: preserve extra repo args, add regression coverage, align package/extension version and Node engine, refresh pm workspace directories, and re-run real fleet policy/test checks.

Real pm install surfaced manifest validation failure: command flags metadata requires schema capability. Added schema to manifest and regression coverage so package-level tests catch the install-time contract.
Fixed multi-repo path parsing, aligned pm-ops runtime metadata, added manifest capability regression coverage, verified real pm install and CLI policy command.

Manual fleet testing found that pm ops policy --repos /path/a /path/b only processed the first repo because remaining path tokens were not folded into the repo list. Also found pm-ops still declared Node >=20 while the pm CLI fleet requires >=22.18.0. Scope: preserve extra repo args, add regression coverage, align package/extension version and Node engine, refresh pm workspace directories, and re-run real fleet policy/test checks.

Real pm install surfaced manifest validation failure: command flags metadata requires schema capability. Added schema to manifest and regression coverage so package-level tests catch the install-time contract.

Release gate exposed npm audit EALLOWSCRIPTS from inherited user npm config. Hardened audit:prod with env -u npm_config_allow_scripts npm_config_userconfig=/dev/null before npm audit.

Addressed prior bot findings that affect fleet report trust: spawn errors/offline mode no longer look like clean outdated/audit results, JSON --output writes JSON and creates parent dirs, test subprocesses have timeouts, and real-data tests can use PM_OPS_TEST_REPOS.

Fourth Greptile pass found verify-release markdown could break on multi-line errors and audit aggregate hid unknown checks. Collapsed error whitespace for markdown cells and added audit summary.unknown plus markdown/test coverage.

Fifth Greptile pass found ops outdated could print a clean footer when checks were unknown. Added unknown guard and regression assertion so the footer appears only after fully checked clean repos.

Sixth Greptile pass found pm workspace probes needed a fallback when pm is not on PATH and missing repo paths lacked explicit scan errors. Restored cached pm invocation fallback and missing-directory scan diagnostics with coverage.

Addressed Greptile summary carryovers: restored simple ~ and glob repo expansion, tsconfig extends traversal, pm item caching, clean outdated count=0, dependency-bucket pm-changelog detection, severity override propagation, no-release-script failure, multiline private runner detection, and npm error message parsing.

Seventh Greptile pass found Windows-only gaps in new glob/test paths. Fixed drive-root handling in expandSimpleGlob and used pm.cmd for test fixture pm spawns on Windows.

Eighth Greptile pass found workflow file reads could abort policy runs and npm-package tsconfig extends resolution was naive. Guarded workflow readFileSync and switched package extends resolution to createRequire fallback.

Tenth Greptile pass found TS 5 array extends could crash strict detection and multiline runs-on scanning only inspected the first block. Added array extends handling and per-runs-on multiline scanning, with array extends covered by fixture.

Greptile follow-up fixed flow-mapping runs-on labels, including { labels: [ubuntu-latest] } violations and { labels: [self-hosted, ubuntu-latest] } self-hosted acceptance. Full release check passes with 35 tests.

Cleanup from bot carryovers: replaced policy fallback array-index lookup with named constants and corrected README install owner slug to unbraind. Full release check still passes with 35 tests.

Manual fleet testing found that pm ops policy --repos /path/a /path/b only processed the first repo because remaining path tokens were not folded into the repo list. Also found pm-ops still declared Node >=20 while the pm CLI fleet requires >=22.18.0. Scope: preserve extra repo args, add regression coverage, align package/extension version and Node engine, refresh pm workspace directories, and re-run real fleet policy/test checks.

Real pm install surfaced manifest validation failure: command flags metadata requires schema capability. Added schema to manifest and regression coverage so package-level tests catch the install-time contract.

Release gate exposed npm audit EALLOWSCRIPTS from inherited user npm config. Hardened audit:prod with env -u npm_config_allow_scripts npm_config_userconfig=/dev/null before npm audit.

Addressed prior bot findings that affect fleet report trust: spawn errors/offline mode no longer look like clean outdated/audit results, JSON --output writes JSON and creates parent dirs, test subprocesses have timeouts, and real-data tests can use PM_OPS_TEST_REPOS.

Greptile pre-push review found Windows command shim/dev-null and JSONC tsconfig regressions. Restored .cmd shim resolution, os.devNull, JSONC tsconfig parsing, pm list timeout, and JSONC fixture coverage.

Second Greptile pass found runReleaseCheck dropped spawnSync error messages and tests embedded local default repos. Surfaced r.error.message in release checks and made PM_OPS_TEST_REPOS opt-in with comma/colon support; local release gate still runs with real repos via env.

Third Greptile pass found strict policy output could be lost before throwing and outdated markdown hid unknown checks. Wrote strict policy details to stdout before throwing and rendered count:null outdated results as explicit unable-to-check sections.

Fourth Greptile pass found verify-release markdown could break on multi-line errors and audit aggregate hid unknown checks. Collapsed error whitespace for markdown cells and added audit summary.unknown plus markdown/test coverage.

Fifth Greptile pass found ops outdated could print a clean footer when checks were unknown. Added unknown guard and regression assertion so the footer appears only after fully checked clean repos.

Sixth Greptile pass found pm workspace probes needed a fallback when pm is not on PATH and missing repo paths lacked explicit scan errors. Restored cached pm invocation fallback and missing-directory scan diagnostics with coverage.

Addressed Greptile summary carryovers: restored simple ~ and glob repo expansion, tsconfig extends traversal, pm item caching, clean outdated count=0, dependency-bucket pm-changelog detection, severity override propagation, no-release-script failure, multiline private runner detection, and npm error message parsing.

Seventh Greptile pass found Windows-only gaps in new glob/test paths. Fixed drive-root handling in expandSimpleGlob and used pm.cmd for test fixture pm spawns on Windows.

Eighth Greptile pass found workflow file reads could abort policy runs and npm-package tsconfig extends resolution was naive. Guarded workflow readFileSync and switched package extends resolution to createRequire fallback.

Ninth Greptile pass found scan/status still used devDependencies-only pm-changelog detection. Added shared hasPmChangelogDep helper and scan coverage for dependencies.

Tenth Greptile pass found TS 5 array extends could crash strict detection and multiline runs-on scanning only inspected the first block. Added array extends handling and per-runs-on multiline scanning, with array extends covered by fixture.

Eleventh Greptile pass found verify-release missing paths reported as no release script and multiline runs-on still missed empty runs-on lines. Restored missing-directory release diagnostic and allowed multiline runs-on blocks with blank same-line value.

Latest Greptile pass found private runner scanning could false-positive on adjacent YAML. Restricted multiline runs-on detection to the indented runs-on value block and added a regression test with a fake gh private-repo response.

Greptile follow-up fixed status collection for missing repo paths and changed tsconfig array extends strict detection to respect later override entries. Added regression tests for both cases.

Greptile follow-up fixed private runner policy precision: self-hosted group/label metadata can include platform words without being misclassified, while direct GitHub-hosted scalar/list runs-on values are still flagged. Added pass/fail fixtures.

GitHub Greptile review found Windows drive-qualified globs could walk C:\ twice. Fixed expandSimpleGlob to preserve drive-qualified absolute inputs and drop the drive token from path segments before expansion.

Review follow-up fixed private runs-on object labels parsing, malformed bracket glob escaping, and README shell fence language hints. Full release check now passes with 34 tests including real-data repos.

Greptile follow-up fixed multiline self-hosted runs-on labels: label arrays are evaluated as a set so self-hosted suppresses platform label false positives, while labels without self-hosted still fail. Full release check passes with 34 tests.

Greptile follow-up fixed flow-mapping runs-on labels, including { labels: [ubuntu-latest] } violations and { labels: [self-hosted, ubuntu-latest] } self-hosted acceptance. Full release check passes with 35 tests.

Cleanup from bot carryovers: replaced policy fallback array-index lookup with named constants and corrected README install owner slug to unbraind. Full release check still passes with 35 tests.

Cubic follow-up fixed cross-platform audit:prod with inline Node env sanitization, removed uppercase npm allow-scripts leakage in runSync, added bracket character-class glob expansion coverage, and made real-data assertions independent of repo names. Full release check passes with 36 tests.

value.author: unknown
value.text:
## Fleet PR finishing pass (session 2026-07-10, PR #6)

**Survivor of #5/#6 supersession review.** Compared PR #5 (`fix/manifest-schema-capability`, v2026.7.5) and PR #6 (`feat/agent-enhancement-pass`, v2026.7.6) against `origin/main` (v2026.7.7, no `schema` capability). #6 contains #5's release-blocking fix (adds `schema` to `capabilities`, verified all 7 commands activate with `failed_count: 0`). #5 closed as superseded by #6; #5's `manifest_version:2`/`permissions` extras are not needed — #6 prevents the EALLOWSCRIPTS subprocess concern at the code level (`runSync` env sanitization + cross-platform `audit:prod`). ops-tg86 (#5's tracker item) closed as superseded-by-ops-f4vp.

### Build/test
- `npm ci && npm run build && npm test`: **34 pass / 0 fail / 2 skipped** (real-data, opt-in via `PM_OPS_TEST_REPOS`). Typecheck clean.

### Functional verification (pm 2026.7.10 host)
- **Load-time safety**: `timeout 20 pm --help` → exit 0 (no fork-bomb/hang). `pm ops --help` lists all 7 subcommands.
- **Activation**: `pm extension install <local-clone> --project` → `runtime_activation_status: ok`, `failed_count: 0`, 7 commands + 7 actions registered. (Note: a stale **global** pm-ops install of #5's v2026.7.5 build was shadowing the project install at runtime; uninstalled it via `pm extension uninstall pm-ops --global` before clean verification.)
- **New fleet commands** (`ops status`/`outdated`/`audit`) exercised with real fixture data + the existing `scan`/`policy`/`verify-release`/`report`:
  - `--format markdown` tables, `--json`, `--output` (incl. nested dir auto-create) all produce correct output; `--format json --output` writes valid JSON (confirmed by parsing), `--format markdown --output` writes markdown. `mkdirSync(dirname, {recursive:true})` confirmed.
  - **Read-only fleet sweep** against 3 real clones (`pm-csv`, `pm-github`, `pm-changelog`): `ops status` (2/3 ready, 1 issue), `ops outdated` (10 total outdated across 3), `ops audit` (3 clean). `git status --porcelain` clean in all 3 clones afterward → genuinely read-only.
  - **Malformed input**: nonexistent repo path → structured `repository directory does not exist` result (exit 0, graceful); bad `--policy <missing>.json` → clean `CommandError` exit 2 (USAGE, no stack trace / no double-invocation); repo with no release gate → `ops verify-release` exits 1 with concise `FAIL <repo>: <step>: <reason>`.
  - **Defaults unchanged vs main**: in-process structured diff (offline) of `ops scan`/`ops policy` default output = byte-identical to `origin/main`; `ops report` differs only by the volatile `generated_at` timestamp. (main v2026.7.7 lacks `schema` so it cannot activate on pm 2026.7.10 — the very blocker this PR fixes — hence the comparison was done by calling `run()` directly like the test harness.)
- **Pre-existing caveat (NOT a #6 regression)**: the `--repos` flag is documented as "comma-separated or repeatable" (inherited verbatim from main), but the pm host maps `list:true` to a single-value option, so `--repos a --repos b` keeps only the last value; the **comma** form `--repos a,b` works correctly. Same wording/example exists in main. Left unchanged to avoid scope creep; documented here.

### Bot review findings (47 inline comments across gemini/greptile/cubic/coderabbit)
Nearly all were already addressed in the final hardening commit `abff8f8` ("fix(ops): harden fleet reports and extension activation"): Windows `.cmd` shims, JSONC tsconfig parsing, `existsSync` guards, `isOffline()` guards on audit/outdated, clean-outdated→0 (not `?`), spawn-error→null, empty-release-gate→fail, `emitResult`/`verify-release` `--format json --output` writing JSON + `mkdirSync` parent creation, `npm_config_allow_scripts` case-insensitive stripping, cross-platform `audit:prod`, bracket-glob char classes, Windows drive-root globs, private-no-runners object/flow/multiline `runs-on` forms, README `unbraind` owner slug, version (2026.7.6), engines (`>=22.18.0`). All confirmed against current code + passing tests. Two legitimately still-applicable findings fixed in this pass:

1. **cubic 3533412804** — real-data test assertion message hardcoded the stale name `pm-ts-starter` after `REAL_REPOS` became env-configurable. Fixed: assertion now interpolates `${REAL_REPOS[1]}`.
2. **cubic 3533514239** — `expandSimpleGlob` returned `readdirSync` order (nondeterministic across filesystems), making fleet reports non-diff-friendly. Fixed: sort expanded matches (`[...candidates].sort()`); glob tests already assert via `.sort()` so no regression. Verified deterministic order (`pm-alpha, pm-mid, pm-zeta`).

One acknowledged-but-not-changed: **cubic 3533412803** (P2, repeated setup boilerplate across the 4 private-runner tests) — valid stylistic note; left explicit for test readability/stability, not a correctness issue.

`npm run changelog:check` → "Changelog is up to date" (exit 0).
